name: Pearl Harbor
description: December 7, 1941 — the surprise attack that brought the United States into World War II.

questions.q: On what date did the attack on Pearl Harbor take place?
Which nation launched the surprise attack on Pearl Harbor?
On which Hawaiian island is Pearl Harbor located?
What is the original Hawaiian name for Pearl Harbor, meaning 'Waters of Pearl'?

source.original_a: Wai Momi (meaning "Waters of Pearl") or Puʻuloa

questions.q: Which Japanese admiral planned the attack on Pearl Harbor?

source.notes: Yamamoto opposed war with the U.S. but planned the attack to cripple the Pacific Fleet.

questions.q: Which admiral commanded the Japanese carrier strike force ('Kidō Butai') during the Pearl Harbor attack?

source.notes: Vice Admiral Nagumo led the six-carrier strike force.

questions.q: From how many aircraft carriers did Japan launch the Pearl Harbor attack?

source.notes: Akagi, Kaga, Soryu, Hiryu, Shokaku, Zuikaku.

questions.q: Approximately how many Japanese aircraft participated in the attack?
About how long did the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor last?
Approximately how many Americans were killed in the Pearl Harbor attack?
Which battleship was sunk with massive loss of life and is now memorialized at Pearl Harbor?

source.notes: Over 1,100 sailors died on the Arizona alone. The USS Arizona Memorial sits above the wreck.

questions.q: Why did the U.S. Pacific Fleet's aircraft carriers escape damage at Pearl Harbor?
choices: They were already on their way to Japan
They were at sea on training exercises and missions
They had been moved to mainland California

source.original_a: They were at sea at the time of the attack
source.notes: USS Enterprise, Lexington, and Saratoga were elsewhere — a strategic blessing for the U.S.

questions.q: Which U.S. president delivered the famous 'a date which will live in infamy' speech the day after the attack?

source.notes: Delivered to a Joint Session of Congress on December 8, 1941.

questions.q: What did the U.S. Congress do hours after FDR's December 8 address?
choices: Declared war on the Axis powers as a whole

source.notes: Germany and Italy declared war on the U.S. three days later (December 11).

questions.q: Which Japanese fighter, the primary air-cover plane at Pearl Harbor, became iconic for its agility?
What special weapon, modified for Pearl Harbor's shallow water, did the Japanese drop from torpedo bombers?

source.notes: Wooden fins added so the torpedoes wouldn't dive too deep into the harbor's mud.

questions.q: Which U.S. destroyer fired the first U.S. shots of the day, sinking a Japanese midget submarine before the air attack?

source.notes: Engaged the midget sub at the harbor entrance about an hour before the air strike.

questions.q: How many U.S. battleships were moored at 'Battleship Row' on the morning of the attack?

source.notes: All eight were hit; four were sunk (Arizona, Oklahoma, California, West Virginia), four damaged.

questions.q: What did U.S. radar operators on Oahu mistake the inbound Japanese strike force for?
choices: Six B-17 bombers en route from California

source.original_a: They were mistaken for six B-17 bombers from the United States
source.notes: The radar contact was real but dismissed by a junior officer.

questions.q: Which Pearl Harbor facilities — repair yards and oil tanks — went largely untouched, allowing the U.S. to recover quickly?
choices: Navy repair yards and oil storage farms

source.original_a: The navy repair yards and oil storage facilities
source.notes: A planned third wave was canceled by Nagumo, sparing this critical infrastructure — a long-debated decision.
